data.description:
## Context

DSO plugin users span two contexts: maintainer projects with PR-based GitHub Ruleset enforcement (this repo, set up by epic 3aaa-0238) and quick-start projects bootstrapped via create-dso-app.sh for non-technical users that may not have a GitHub remote. Today merge-to-main.sh hard-codes a direct git push origin main that is rejected by the Ruleset — every merge from this repo's worktrees is currently broken (surfaced by the 2026-04-27 retro of 3aaa-0238). The plugin has no project-level switch for whether the commit workflow runs local test/review or relies on CI. This epic ships configuration infrastructure (two new config keys), the PR-based merge implementation with autonomous review-thread resolution validated by feasibility spike, the ruleset-rule knobs that match this repo's manual settings, the COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md restructure with a one-time auditor sub-agent dispatch, and updates to onboarding / installer / template defaults so each project type gets the right behavior.

## Success Criteria

- Config keys merge.strategy (direct|pr, default direct) and enforcement.strategy (local|ci|both, default local) introduced; merge-to-main.sh becomes a thin dispatcher that execs merge-to-main-direct.sh (extracted current behavior) or merge-to-main-pr.sh (new); PreToolUse hooks and .pre-commit-config.yaml entries read enforcement.strategy and short-circuit to no-op (with structured HOOK_GATE: skipped reason=enforcement.strategy=ci log) when value is ci.
- COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md restructured: a Step 0.6 Load enforcement profile reads enforcement.strategy and conditionally loads commit-workflow-validation.md (extracted local-validation steps including format, lint, type-check, the test-status recording call, and the review gate) when value is local or both; skips the load when ci.
- Running merge-to-main.sh with merge.strategy=pr against the live digital-service-orchestra repo creates a PR via gh pr create, queues gh pr merge --auto, polls CI plus threads, programmatically resolves threads via the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation after addressing each in code or via REST reply, and exits zero after the merge commit lands on origin/main — verified in-session via gh run list and git log origin/main.
- Required-checks blocking is verified in-session on a real PR: a deliberate test PR with gh pr merge --auto queued; in-session checks confirm the PR remains in BLOCKED mergeStateStatus until each required check (CI tests, LLM review, validate-ci, platform-matrix legs, pipefail-strict, conversation-resolution) reports a green conclusion; gh pr merge --squash rejects with not mergeable until all checks pass; the PR auto-merges only after the final check turns green.
- A dedicated story carries the COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md restructure; the story description embeds two audit criteria — (a) no enforcement bypass: every former gate's enforcement is preserved by either the conditional load OR the hook-gating, with no path that lets unreviewed/untested code reach a commit when enforcement.strategy=local; (b) no unnecessary friction: the ci path is leaner than the local path, the local path adds no new steps over the pre-epic baseline, the conditional load adds no more than 1 net Bash call. The story closes only when the standard review/remediation cycle reports zero important findings against these criteria; orchestrators are prohibited from closing the story while any important finding remains open.
- provision-ruleset.sh extended with config knobs matching the manual rules on this repo: bypass_actor_policy (always|pull_request_only), require_conversation_resolution, request_copilot_review, dismiss_stale_approvals_on_push, required_approvals; CodeQL and code-quality rules are deferred to a follow-on placeholder epic.
- /dso:onboarding asks the user to choose between direct and pr (defaulting to direct for non-technical users) and writes the choice to dso-config.conf; selecting pr triggers provision-ruleset.sh automatically.
- ci-generator.sh and ci-skeleton-templates.md gain a sibling PR-mode template variant exposed via ci-generator.sh --mode=pr-protected; the generated workflow includes the Resolve-and-fetch DSO plugin assets step from the CI parity epic.
- create-dso-app.sh writes merge.strategy=direct and enforcement.strategy=local to the new project's dso-config.conf and does NOT provision a Ruleset; the digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template repo's checked-in dso-config.conf matches.
- This project's dso-config.conf is updated within this epic to set merge.strategy=pr and enforcement.strategy=local; a directional note is added to the operational-flip epic that enforcement.strategy flips to ci when that epic runs.
- plugins/dso/scripts/onboarding/github-bootstrap.sh updated so its git push origin HEAD:main escape hatch is conditional on merge.strategy=direct OR an as-yet-unprovisioned ruleset; on PR-mode projects with a pre-existing ruleset, the script either uses the admin bypass when configured or surfaces a clear error directing the user to disable the ruleset for the bootstrap window.
- merge-to-main-direct.sh and merge-to-main-pr.sh both emit CONFLICT_DATA output with the same schema on conflict, OR merge-to-main-pr.sh documents that conflicts are surfaced via a different signal — plugins/dso/skills/resolve-conflicts/SKILL.md is updated to handle whichever shape is chosen; verified by a regression test.
- Happy-path E2E: a real merge-to-main.sh --strategy=pr run on this repo's worktree completes autonomously — PR created, CI passes, threads resolved, branch merged, script exits 0; verified in-session via git log origin/main showing the merge commit.
- Direct-mode regression: on a project with merge.strategy=direct and no GitHub remote, merge-to-main.sh behaves identically to the pre-epic baseline — no new prompts, no new errors, same exit code.
- Thread-resolution path: a deliberately seeded blocking review thread on a test PR is resolved by the autonomous loop within the wait window, OR (negative test) a thread the agent cannot programmatically resolve causes the loop to escalate to the user with the PR URL rather than spinning indefinitely.

## Dependencies

Blocked by the CI LLM-review parity epic (must ship first so enforcement.strategy=ci is a real fidelity-equivalent option, not a downgrade). Soft-blocks the operational-flip epic (re-scoped 1083-fb3d).

## Approach

Two-script split: extract current merge-to-main.sh body into merge-to-main-direct.sh (no behavior change). Implement merge-to-main-pr.sh using gh pr create + gh pr merge --auto + a wait loop. merge-to-main.sh becomes a thin dispatcher that reads merge.strategy and execs the matching script. PR-mode wait loop polls CI plus threads at 30s cadence (configurable); each iteration fetches CI conclusions and unresolved thread node IDs in two parallel gh calls. Unresolved threads dispatch a general-purpose sonnet sub-agent (with prompt template at plugins/dso/scripts/prompts/pr-comment-responder.md — a prompt template, NOT a registered agent file) per thread to address each, then call resolveReviewThread. Settling heuristic: declare done only when CI green AND zero unresolved threads AND no new threads in last 120s. Hook gating implemented as an early enforcement.strategy check at the top of each PreToolUse hook and .pre-commit-config.yaml entry. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md extracts validation steps into commit-workflow-validation.md. Ruleset knobs extend provision-ruleset.sh with the 5 named knobs.

## Scenario Analysis

- PR-mode wait loop spins indefinitely when a required check is permanently broken (critical, runtime).
- resolveReviewThread GraphQL mutation called on an already-resolved thread causes silent loop divergence (high, runtime).
- Concurrent worktree sessions both dispatch merge-to-main-pr.sh and create duplicate PRs (high, runtime).
- enforcement.strategy=ci causes all local hooks to skip, leaving the commit gate open during a network partition (high, runtime).
- merge.strategy=pr requires gh CLI with GraphQL support; version mismatch causes opaque failure deep in the wait loop (high, deployment).
- ci-generator.sh --mode=pr-protected template adds a conversation_resolution required check but validate-required-checks.sh does not know about the new check context name (medium, deployment).
- enforcement.strategy=both causes double-execution of the local validation steps (record-status and review-gate) producing conflicting gate artifacts (high, configuration).
- merge.strategy=pr with enforcement.strategy=ci and no GitHub remote causes silent no-op commit workflow with no error (high, configuration).
- COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md conditional load of commit-workflow-validation.md fails when enforcement.strategy key is absent from dso-config.conf — default-fallback handling depends on config-reader implementation (medium, configuration).

## Spike Findings

Verified 2026-04-28 against GitHub GraphQL API v4 docs and 3 prior-art examples. PR review-thread resolution is GraphQL-only via the resolveReviewThread mutation; thread node IDs (PRRT_kwDO...) come from the reviewThreads query on a PullRequest, NOT from REST /pulls/{n}/comments. Replies to review comments DO use REST: POST /repos/{o}/{r}/pulls/{n}/comments/{id}/replies. Mixed REST/GraphQL is the expected pattern. gh CLI has no native subcommand for resolution (cli/cli#12419) — use gh api graphql -f query=... Most common failure: missing contents: write token scope returns errors embedded in HTTP 200 GraphQL response body. Auto-merge waits for resolved threads ONLY when the Require conversation resolution before merging branch-protection rule is enabled — confirmed manually enabled on this repo. Prior art verified: agynio/gh-pr-review extension, corylanou Claude Code skill gist, kieranklaassen resolve-pr-comments gist — all use the same query-then-mutate pattern.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (gh CLI, GitHub GraphQL, GitHub Ruleset API, Anthropic claude-code-action), security/credentials (Ruleset bypass actors, token scopes)
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 9
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): triggered — cycles: 1, gap: PR review-thread resolution mechanism (resolved via 2026-04-28 spike against 3 prior-art examples)
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md restructure; onboarding/SKILL.md question addition)
- Scale context (Step 0): small scale (default) — per-merge / per-PR invocation; not volume-sensitive
data.title: PR-merge mode and enforcement-strategy config: configurable merge-to-main with autonomous PR-comment resolution
